authoritative_sources.title: Selling on the Internet: Prompt Delivery Rules
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection authority for unfair or deceptive commerce practices. Its prompt-delivery guidance governs internet order timing promises, delay notices, cancellation rights, prompt refunds, and order-record discipline.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: order acceptance, fulfillment promise control, delay handling, cancellation packaging, and refund routing
used_for: online grocery order promise boundaries
delay and cancellation workflow expectations
prompt-refund escalation posture when fulfillment commitments fail

authoritative_sources.title: NIST Handbook 130 - Uniform Laws and Regulations in the Areas of Legal Metrology and Fuel Quality
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST Office of Weights and Measures publishes the public legal-metrology baseline used by many jurisdictions for method-of-sale, unit pricing, e-commerce, and price-verification controls. It is authoritative for grocery weighted-item, unit-price, shelf/POS/online price, and quantity representation discipline.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: weighted-item price review, unit-price evidence handling, online-price conflict handling, and price-verification escalation
used_for: PLU, catch-weight, variable-price, and unit-price evidence
online versus POS or advertised-price conflict handling
price-verification and legal-metrology source hierarchy

authoritative_sources.title: 2022 FDA Food Code
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA Food Code is the primary U.S. model code for retail food and food-service safety and explicitly includes delivery of grocery orders as an indirect consumer handoff. It is authoritative for safe food handling, honest presentation, labeling, source, and time or temperature control concepts that constrain grocery e-commerce fulfillment.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: perishable handling, cold-chain staging, food presentation, labeling, and safety stop-condition review
used_for: cold-chain and temperature-abuse escalation
food-safety stop conditions
safe and honest presentation constraints for picked and substituted items

authoritative_sources.title: Recalls, Market Withdrawals, & Safety Alerts
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA is the primary federal authority for FDA-regulated product recall, withdrawal, and safety alert listings. The source is authoritative for current recall facts, reasons, product descriptions, company ownership, and status signals that constrain grocery e-commerce release and customer-impact routing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: recall check, stop-sale, staged-order release prevention, and customer-impact escalation
used_for: recall and allergen stop conditions
item isolation and release refusal posture
customer-impact evidence requirements

authoritative_sources.title: Retailer Criteria to Provide Online Purchasing to SNAP Households
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FNS administers SNAP retailer authorization and is authoritative for online SNAP retailer participation requirements, secure PIN-entry, split tender, eligible-food boundaries, error handling, refunds, variable or estimated-price handling, and fraud controls.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: SNAP EBT order validation, split-tender handling, estimated-price review, refund packaging, and SNAP-sensitive exception routing
used_for: SNAP online authorization and transaction evidence
split-tender and eligible-food boundary handling
SNAP-sensitive refund and fraud escalation triggers

authoritative_sources.title: Online Purchasing for Retailers - Technical Questions and Answers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FNS technical Q&As are authoritative for online grocery SNAP operations because they directly address substitutions, weighted-item adjustments, PIN-less refunds, prohibitions on cash or gift-card refunds for SNAP purchases, online order minimums, equal prices for SNAP customers, split tender, and remote card or PIN handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: substitution, weighted-item refund, PIN-less refund, and SNAP-sensitive exception handling
used_for: substitution and weighted-price edge cases
SNAP refund method boundaries
remote EBT card and PIN handling refusal triggers

authoritative_sources.title: Best Practices for Securing E-commerce
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCI SSC is the recognized standards body for payment-card security. Its e-commerce guidance is authoritative for merchant responsibility, third-party payment provider boundaries, checkout data protection, and payment-risk evidence expectations in grocery e-commerce transactions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment-risk review, account takeover signal handling, third-party payment handoff, and fraud/loss-prevention escalation
used_for: merchant versus provider responsibility boundaries
e-commerce payment-risk evidence expectations
fraud-sensitive checkout and payment escalation posture

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 General Specifications Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Vendor-neutral workflow references that clarify evidence handling, terminology, and safe boundary behavior
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 is the recognized global standards body for retail identifiers and data carriers. Its General Specifications are authoritative for GTIN, UPC, barcode, lot, batch, date, and product-identity handling across POS, OMS, inventory, recall, and e-commerce fulfillment records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: item identity, barcode, batch, date-code, and recall-record linkage review
used_for: SKU, UPC, GTIN, lot, date, and barcode terminology
record linkage across POS, inventory, OMS, and recall systems
evidence discipline for substitutions and affected-item checks

workflow_stages.description: Confirm retailer, store, channel, customer type, order or basket identifier, fulfillment window, payment mix, and tenant authority context before accepting work and classifying it as in scope, blocked, or out of scope.

workflow_stages.description: Move price conflicts, weighted-item adjustments, refund exceptions, split-tender mismatch, SNAP-sensitive questions, coupon disputes, chargeback indicators, and payment-risk issues into blocked or escalated states with owner routing.

workflow_stages.description: Stop routine handling when recall, undeclared allergen, temperature-abuse, contamination, fraud, account-takeover, or loss-prevention signals change whether the order can be picked, released, refunded, or closed.

workflow_stages.description: Only close work when pick, substitution, refund, staging, release, delivery, blocked-item, and escalation evidence has been recorded and tenant-specific rules have been applied without overriding the public baseline.

artifacts: online grocery orders, baskets, carts, customer profiles, pickup or delivery windows, and service tickets
POS receipts, OMS events, e-commerce platform events, payment gateway records, SNAP EBT records, split-tender details, refund records, coupons, promotions, and service-recovery notes
SKU, UPC, GTIN, PLU, lot, batch, date-code, sell-by, use-by, catch-weight, variable-price, unit-price, and per-pound-price records

picker notes, substitution approvals or rejections, short-pick records, out-of-stock notes, inventory reservations, staging-zone scans, cold-chain or temperature-control notes, delivery handoff records, and customer acknowledgments
recall notices, allergen alerts, food-safety complaints, contamination notes, chargeback indicators, suspicious-account signals, fraud or loss-prevention holds, and escalation packets
the lane's execution status updates, exception summaries, completion evidence records, policy-conflict summaries, and adjacent-owner handoff packages
systems_of_record: SNAP EBT processor or retailer SNAP online records
pickup, delivery, or last-mile management system
food-safety, recall, quality, or incident repository

allowed: Classify grocery e-commerce requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Advance, hold, rework, or complete online grocery work based on documented lifecycle state and evidence.
Prepare evidence-backed execution summaries, exception notes, completion records, and escalation packages without overstating authority.
Apply documented substitution, pick, staging, release, and completion rules when the governing records are present and consistent.
escalate: Required order, payment, SNAP, item, inventory, substitution, staging, delivery, recall, safety, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.

The request requires pricing override, refund exception approval, SNAP program interpretation, fraud adjudication, food-safety disposition, recall clearance, legal interpretation, or executive approval.
Public guidance and tenant, platform, franchise, payment, state, or jurisdictional policy materially conflict.
Customer harm, fraud, food safety, recall, allergen, payment-risk, or policy exception determines whether execution may proceed.
refuse: Mark work complete without pick, substitution, refund, staging, release, delivery, or blocked-item evidence.
Override pricing, promotion, SNAP, refund, or safety controls without documented authority.
Release recalled, allergen-impacted, temperature-abused, food-safety-questioned, fraud-held, or payment-unresolved product to pickup, delivery, or saleable inventory.
Accept remote EBT card or PIN details, issue prohibited refund methods, or improvise SNAP policy decisions.

domain_failure_modes: OMS, POS, SNAP EBT, and pickup records conflict and the lane releases a grocery order anyway
weighted-item, PLU, or per-pound price evidence is missing and the lane approves a refund or price adjustment outside delegated authority
a substitution materially changes allergen, dietary, SNAP-eligible, promotional, or price conditions without customer or policy evidence
a recalled or undeclared-allergen item remains in a staged basket and is released because completion pressure overrides stop-sale handling
temperature-abuse or contamination complaints are treated as normal service recovery rather than food-safety escalation
fraud or account-takeover signals are handled as ordinary customer friction and the lane bypasses loss-prevention routing
high-volume weather or holiday queues collapse distinct exception classes into generic completion
tenant-specific platform, franchise, or jurisdiction rules are ignored in favor of a generic retail-commerce answer

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline

unresolved_ambiguities.handling: validated operation requires tenant and jurisdiction retrieval before final price, unit-price, refund, or substitution outcomes are stated. The lane records conflict and escalates when public and tenant sources materially differ.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific SNAP online authorization and platform implementation
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The public FNS baseline constrains SNAP-sensitive handling. Runtime must retrieve tenant authorization, EBT processor behavior, platform integration, refund workflow, and approved split-tender rules before advancing SNAP-sensitive exceptions.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Local food-code adoption and cold-chain operating thresholds
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: FDA Food Code is a model baseline. Runtime must retrieve adopted local rules and tenant temperature-control policies; missing or conflicting safety context causes escalation rather than in-lane disposition.

notes: The public source set covers workflow, evidence, terminology, pricing/refund/payment constraints, SNAP-sensitive handling, food safety, recall, fraud, and loss-prevention boundaries.
The final spec pack constrains grocery e-commerce execution to evidence-backed lifecycle handling and explicit escalation rather than pricing, refund, SNAP, fraud, food-safety, or recall authority.
